title: CRM Sync Without Copy-Paste for Consulting Firms
metaDescription: Stop retyping client data between inbox, calendar, and CRM. Ayothedoc installs a live CRM sync for consulting firms so every record stays current automatically.
excerpt: A done-for-you CRM sync for consulting firms. Your inbox, calendar, and CRM stay aligned on live data, so nobody has to copy-paste contact notes ever again.

sections.intro:
Consulting firms run on relationships, and those relationships are scattered across inboxes, calendar invites, proposal docs, and a CRM that nobody loves updating. Partners take notes in email threads, associates log meetings in their heads, and by Friday the CRM is a week behind reality. When a partner asks who last spoke to a prospect, the honest answer is usually a guess.

The AIOS fixes this at the plumbing level. We install a live connection between the systems you already use so contacts, meetings, and conversation context land in the CRM on their own. No bulk imports on Sunday night. No associate spending three hours a week on data hygiene. The CRM becomes a reflection of what actually happened, not a chore.
sections.fourCFit:
This page sits in the Connections layer of the AIOS. Connections is the layer that lets the system read and write across your tools using live data, so every other layer has something true to work from.

  • Context: knows your service lines, partners, and how you categorize accounts.
  • Connections: this capability, keeping inbox, calendar, billing, and CRM in sync.
  • Capabilities: drafting follow-ups, summarizing calls, and routing leads on top of clean CRM data.
  • Cadence: a scheduled sweep that reconciles anything the live sync missed.

Without Connections, the rest of the AIOS guesses. With it, every workflow downstream runs on current facts.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
• New contacts from your inbox and calendar appear in the CRM within 5 minutes of first contact, with source, owner, and account assigned.
  • Every external meeting on a partner or associate calendar creates or updates a CRM activity record, with attendees matched to existing contacts.
  • Email threads with prospects and clients are linked to the right account, with a short summary written into the CRM activity note.
  • Contact fields (title, company, phone, LinkedIn) update from email signatures and calendar invites, with conflicts flagged for review rather than silently overwritten.
  • A weekly reconciliation report shows duplicates, missing owners, and stalled opportunities so the team can act on real gaps.
  • Service level: live events sync in under 5 minutes, the reconciliation sweep runs nightly, and the audit report lands in your inbox every Monday at 7am.
sections.howWeInstall:
1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes). We look at how your CRM is structured today, where data actually lives, and which fields matter to partners. Run the audit to start.
  2. Install (10 business days). We map your inbox, calendar, and CRM, build the sync rules to your taxonomy, set up the duplicate and conflict handling, and run a quiet two-day shadow period before going live. Partners approve the field mapping before anything writes.
  3. Operate (ongoing). The sync runs continuously. We add one new automation per week on top of it, things like proposal status tracking, renewal reminders, or referral source attribution. See plans for what is included.
sections.expectedResults:
• Associates and partners recover 3 to 6 hours per person per week previously spent on CRM upkeep and meeting notes.
  • CRM data freshness improves from days or weeks behind to under 5 minutes for most events.
  • Duplicate contact rate typically drops by 60 to 80 percent within the first month.
  • Pipeline reviews become useful again because the data shown is the data that exists.
  • Standing guarantee: the AIOS recovers 40 or more hours a month across your firm, or we keep working free until it does.
sections.faq:
Will this replace our CRM?

No. We work with whatever CRM you already use. The AIOS is tool-agnostic and sits alongside your stack, making it more accurate, not replacing it.

What happens when the sync sees conflicting information, like two different phone numbers?

Conflicts are never silently overwritten. The system flags them in the weekly reconciliation report with both values and the source, and a human on your team decides which wins. You always own the data.

Can partners opt out of having their calendars read?

Yes. Sync scope is configured per user during install. Some firms include all client-facing staff, others limit it to business development. We set the rules to match how your firm actually operates.
